{
  "judge_id": "Judge-001",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.3": 4,
    "R1.4": 2,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 3,
    "RX.4": 3,
    "RX.5": 1
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The output audits every load-bearing claim against the corresponding table/figure.",
    "R1.4": "The output names one prior work and articulates the delta, but could map against more works for a clearer comparison.",
    "RX.1": "The output flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims.",
    "RX.3": "Mostly within scope with one drift (the panoptic claim).",
    "RX.4": "Output is harm-free but with one minor regression (inconsistent benchmark naming).",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.3": 0.9,
    "R1.4": 0.8,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.6
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The output audits every load-bearing claim against the corresponding table/figure.
rationale.R1.4: The output names one prior work and articulates the delta, but could map against more works for a clearer comparison.
rationale.RX.1: The output flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims.
rationale.RX.3: Mostly within scope with one drift (the panoptic claim).
rationale.RX.4: Output is harm-free but with one minor regression (inconsistent benchmark naming).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason.
